macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Stock Market Rallies on Iran Strait Resolution
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose 1.3% to a new record on Friday following Iran's announcement that the Strait of Hormuz has reopened for commercial traffic. Oil prices dropped roughly 14% to $81 per barrel, easing inflation concerns.

items.title: Fed Official Warns Markets May Undervalue Conflict Risks
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Waller cautioned that securities markets may be underestimating the risk of continued conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure, despite March inflation data showing energy prices jumped 10.8% and headline inflation reaching 3.3%.

items.title: Treasury Yields Fall, Fed Rate Cut Odds Rise
items.summary: The two-year Treasury yield fell about 8 basis points to 3.7% on Friday, with markets pricing in roughly a 50% chance of at least one Fed rate cut by year-end, up from less than 30% the previous day.

items.title: Q1 GDP Growth Moderates to 2.4% Despite Strong Business Investment
items.summary: Real GDP grew at a 2.4% annual rate in the first quarter, supported by continued surge in business investment and data center construction, though consumer spending showed apparent softness.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Liquidations Reach $817M in 24 Hours Amid US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Crypto markets experienced $817 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, primarily $661 million in short positions, driven by risk-off sentiment from US-Iran conflict.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks Two-Month High as Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Bitcoin surged to a two-month high, boosting crypto stocks like Strategy, Robinhood, and Coinbase, following Iran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Surge to $745M Across Major Exchanges
items.summary: Crypto futures saw $745 million in liquidations in the past 24 hours, reflecting heightened market volatility.

items.title: Network-Wide Liquidations Hit $767M, Mostly Shorts
items.summary: Total network liquidations reached $767 million in 24 hours, with $596 million from shorts and $171 million from longs, per Coinglass data.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits $78K, Triggering $820M in Market Liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin rallied above $78,000 to a 10-week high on Friday, causing $820 million in liquidations as markets reacted to cooling US-Israel tensions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: ChatGPT 5.3 Reduces Outbound Links with Stricter Authority Signals in AI Search
items.summary: ChatGPT 5.3 introduces broader query expansion with 10+ fan-out searches per prompt but cites fewer sources, dropping average domains by 20%, while prioritizing authority signals like credentials and awards. This continues the trend of declining referral traffic to publishers.

items.title: OpenAI Memo Reveals Enterprise Platform Strategy Amid Competition with Anthropic
items.summary: OpenAI's chief revenue officer outlines a shift to a unified enterprise AI platform emphasizing multiproduct adoption, agent platforms, and full-stack deployment to boost switching costs. It highlights intense rivalry with Anthropic over compute, platform breadth, and accessibility.

items.title: Canva Acquires Simtheory and Ortto to Build Full Marketing Platform
items.summary: Canva expands beyond design by acquiring agentic AI collaboration platform Simtheory and marketing automation system Ortto, integrating content creation, AI workflows, and campaign execution into one platform.

items.title: Anthropic Prepares Claude Opus 4.7 Release with Full-Stack AI Studio Tools
items.summary: Anthropic advances toward a full-stack AI studio by releasing Claude Opus 4.7 alongside a design tool for generating websites, presentations, and landing pages from natural language prompts, while reserving Mythos as a restricted frontier model.

items.title: DeepSeek V3.2 Emerges as Cost-Effective Open-Source Agentic AI Model
items.summary: DeepSeek V3.2 from China offers near-frontier reasoning at lower cost, open-source for self-hosting and data privacy, ideal for agentic applications in compliance-heavy industries.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Metro 2039 Fully Revealed with Cinematic Trailer
items.summary: Metro 2039 was officially revealed with a cinematic trailer showing some gameplay footage, set for release this winter. The announcement highlights new post-apocalyptic action in the series.

items.title: Cyberpunk TCG Kickstarter Nears $25 Million in Final 24 Hours
items.summary: The Cyberpunk TCG Kickstarter campaign is in its final 24 hours, poised to become the most funded game and TCG in history at $25 million. It previously hit records like fastest to $5 million.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake Leak Suggests July Release
items.summary: Leaks indicate Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake, codenamed 'Resync' or 'Reync', is set for July release with an official reveal expected soon. Insider Gaming reports early details emerging.

items.title: Starfield PS5 Version Faces Backlash with Bugs and Refund Demands
items.summary: Starfield PS5 players report widespread crashes and glitches making the game unplayable, leading to refund demands; Bethesda announced a hotfix about 24 hours ago while Xbox player count rises.

items.title: New 90s Game Console Makes Surprise Return Announcement
items.summary: A new game console reviving a classic 90s system was announced out of nowhere. A major game reveal is expected next week with early leaks.

items.title: Sword Art Online: Iron Clad New Trailer at Galaxy Spring 2026
items.summary: A new trailer for Sword Art Online game 'Iron Clad' (part of Echoes of Aincrad) was released, set for July launch. It coincides with Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake leak.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Meta Raises Prices on Quest VR Headsets Due to AI-Driven Chip Shortages
items.summary: Meta Platforms is increasing U.S. prices for Quest 3S and Quest 3 VR headsets starting April 19, citing surging memory chip costs from AI data-center demand by OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft. Similar price hikes have affected Dell, HP, Microsoft, and Sony's PlayStation 5.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computing by scaling the mobile OS to desktops.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has launched the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: US Senator Warren Raises Concerns Over Nvidia's Acquisition of SchedMD
items.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren has expressed concerns about Nvidia's acquisition of SchedMD, which controls Slurm software used in US government supercomputers, citing national security and competition risks.

items.title: Arlo Acquires Aloe Care Health to Expand into Wellness and Aging-in-Place Tech
items.summary: Arlo Technologies has acquired AI-powered medical alert platform Aloe Care Health to enhance its SaaS offerings for aging in place and caregiver support.

items.title: Sequoia Leaders Raise $7 Billion for Major Tech Investments
items.summary: New leaders at Sequoia have secured about $7 billion for the firm's largest investment bets in tech and internet sectors.

items.title: ScanSource Launches Converged Communications Team and Partners with Arrow McLaren
items.summary: ScanSource has introduced a dedicated Converged Communications CX Team and entered a multi-year partnership with Arrow McLaren to boost channel growth.
